rationale: Snorlax vs Blissey turn 1: Curse / Lovely Kiss are tempting setup lines on Snorlax, but if a Ground-type hard-counter (Rhydon-class) is on the team, the converter line is to pivot in immediately and 2HKO Blissey with Earthquake. Predicting Curse/setup over the hard-counter pivot is the missed_class pattern this probe locks in. Even if Blissey's Toxic lands on the incoming Rhydon, the EV trade is favorable because Rhydon kills Blissey before poison kills Rhydon.
occurrence_pattern: Across the missed_class cluster (4 instances at probe creation), the unifying recipe is: when the pro switched and I predicted a move, the pivot was usually to a hard-counter or a route-changing attacker. The Snorlax->Rhydon vs Blissey case is the cleanest instance because the type matchup is unambiguous.
